content.problemStatement:
You are given a positive integer KKK and a sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã. Initially, each element of this sequence is 000 or 111; your goal is to make all elements equal to 000.

In order to do that, you may perform the following operation any number of times:


Choose a subarray (a contiguous subsequence) with length KKK, i.e. choose two indices LLL and RRR such that 1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N1 \le L \le R \le N1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N and R‚àíL+1=KR-L+1 = KR‚àíL+1=K.
Choose one element of that subarray and change it to 000, i.e. choose an index PPP such that L‚â§P‚â§RL \le P \le RL‚â§P‚â§R and change APA_PAP‚Äã to 000.
The cost of this operation is the sum of this subarray AL+AL+1+‚Ä¶+ARA_L + A_{L+1} + \ldots + A_RAL‚Äã+AL+1‚Äã+‚Ä¶+AR‚Äã before APA_PAP‚Äã is changed.
Find the minimum total cost (sum of costs of individual operations) of making all elements of AAA equal to 000.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 4‚ãÖ1054 \cdot 10^54‚ãÖ105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the minimum cost of turning all elements of AAA into zeros.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The minimum cost is 333 and it can be obtained using the following sequence of operations:

choose the subarray [L,R]=[2,4][L,R] = [2,4][L,R]=[2,4] and set A3=0A_3=0A3‚Äã=0 with cost 111
choose the subarray [1,3][1,3][1,3] and set A1=0A_1=0A1‚Äã=0 with cost 111
choose the subarray [3,5][3,5][3,5] and set A5=0A_5=0A5‚Äã=0 with cost 111

Example case 2: We have to choose the whole sequence ([L,R]=[1,4][L,R] = [1,4][L,R]=[1,4]) in each operation, so the total cost is 3+2+1=63+2+1=63+2+1=6:

choose the subarray [1,4][1,4][1,4] and set A1=0A_1=0A1‚Äã=0 with cost 333
choose the subarray [1,4][1,4][1,4] and set A2=0A_2=0A2‚Äã=0 with cost 222
choose the subarray [1,4][1,4][1,4] and set A4=0A_4=0A4‚Äã=0 with cost 111

Example case 3: The minimum cost is 222 and it can be obtained using the following sequence of operations:

choose the subarray [2,2][2,2][2,2] and set A2=0A_2=0A2‚Äã=0 with cost 111
choose the subarray [1,1][1,1][1,1] and set A1=0A_1=0A1‚Äã=0 with cost 111
